meta.name: LSAS-SR Liebowitz Social Anxiety Scale
meta.description: The LSAS-SR is a 24-item self-report scale that comprehensively assesses fear and anxiety in various social and performance situations. It is one of the most widely used measures of social anxiety disorder severity.
meta.author: Liebowitz (1987)
meta.reference: Liebowitz MR. Social phobia. Mod Probl Pharmacopsychiatry. 1987;22:141-173.
meta.instruction: For each situation below, please rate the level of fear or anxiety you would experience. Select the option that best describes your typical reaction.

questions.text: Drinking with others in public places
Acting, performing, or giving a talk in front of an audience
Calling someone you don't know very well
Talking with people you don't know very well
Entering a room when others are already seated
Expressing a disagreement or disapproval to people you don't know very well
Looking at people you don't know very well in the eyes
Giving a report to a group

ranges.label: No Social Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score suggests no significant social anxiety. You are generally comfortable in social situations.
suggestions: Keep engaging in social activities that you enjoy

ranges.label: Moderate Social Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate social anxiety. You may experience noticeable discomfort in certain social situations.
suggestions: Practice gradual exposure to feared social situations
Learn relaxation techniques such as deep breathing
Consider joining a social skills training group
Challenge negative thoughts about social evaluation

ranges.label: Marked Social Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score indicates marked social anxiety. Social situations likely cause you significant distress and may interfere with daily functioning.
suggestions: Consider seeking professional help from a therapist experienced in social anxiety
Cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT) has strong evidence for social anxiety
Practice systematic desensitization with a professional
Build a support network of understanding friends or family

ranges.label: Severe Social Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score indicates severe social anxiety. Social situations are extremely distressing and likely significantly impair your daily life.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional treatment
A combination of therapy and medication may be most effective
Consider CBT specifically designed for social anxiety disorder
Do not isolate yourself; reach out for support
Remember that social anxiety is highly treatable with proper help
